body:
CRT Abuse in DC Schools!

[INSIDE JW]

DC Schools Pushed Racial Segregation in Employee ‘Affinity Spaces’

[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=59355&L=27640&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvdmlkZW9zL2p1ZGljaWFsLXdhdGNoLXN1ZXMtbnljLXRvLWNsZWFuLXVwLXZvdGVyLXJvbGxzLWJpZGVuLWF0dGFja3Mtc3VwcmVtZS1jb3VydC1jcnQtYWJ1c2UtaW4tZGMvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz]
The school district in your nation’s capital is spending tax
dollars to promote segregation based upon race and sexual identity.

We received 194 pages
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=59355&L=27616&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2RjLXB1YmxpYy1zY2hvb2xzLWFmZmluaXR5LXNwYWNlcy1hcHJpbC0yMDIyLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==]
of records from District of Columbia Public Schools (DCPS) which show
that DC officials pushed segregated “Affinity Spaces” on the basis
of race and sexual identity.

We obtained the records in response to a June 24, 2021, FOIA request
for:

Records identifying the number of affinity spaces hosted by District
of Columbia Public Schools from August 31, 2020 to June 24, 2021.

Records identifying the topics discussed during any affinity spaces
hosted by District of Columbia Public Schools from August 31, 2020 to
June 24, 2021.

Records inviting students, faculty, and staff to affinity spaces
hosted by District of Columbia Public Schools from August 31, 2020 to
June 24, 2021.

Records, including policies and procedures, regarding the creation and
use of “affinity spaces.”

Any analyses of whether affinity spaces excluding students, faculty,
and staff who identify as a specific race or gender is consistent with
district and federal law, including but not limited to 42 U.S.C. 2000d
and the Equal Protection clause of the 14th Amendment of the U.S.
Constitution.
The records include a DCPS September 2021 PowerPoint presentation
titled “DCPS Affinity Group Interest Form
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=59355&L=27617&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2RjLXB1YmxpYy1zY2hvb2xzLWFmZmluaXR5LXNwYWNlcy1hcHJpbC0yMDIyLXBncy0zLTcvXeKAnT91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==
stating:

DCPS staff: The Equity Strategy and Programming Team initially
launched affinity spaces as safe spaces for you to reflect and process
following the murder of George Floyd, and we are going to continue
them throughout the 21-22 school year as a place for folks to reflect
and continue to learn and grow.

One way to process in a safe space is through affinity. Affinity
spaces are gathering opportunities for people who share a common
identity. This space will be organized based on the racial identities
represented in Central Office as we aim to lean into the Courageous
Conversation condition of isolating race.

For more information about the benefit of racial affinity groups,
please leverage this Learning for Justice resource:
https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=59355&L=27618&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubGVhcm5pbmdmb3JqdXN0aWNlLm9yZy9tYWdhemluZS9zdW1tZXItMjAxNS9tYWtpbmctc3BhY2U/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=

DCPS Central Office staff from the Equity, Community Action and SEL
[Social and Emotional Learning] Teams will co-facilitate these
affinity groups in collaboration with volunteers at least once a month
but more frequently as requested by the group.
A form in the presentation asks respondents to submit their pronouns,
which include she/her, she/they, he/him, he/they, they/them, ze/hir,
she/he/they, or “other.”

DCPS staff is asked to select “Which racial affinity group(s) do you
plan to join via Teams? (Select all that apply to you and your racial
identities. A separate calendar/Teams invite will come from the DCPS
Equity calendar.):”

Asian American Pacific Islander

Black/African American, Hispanic/Latinx

Indigenous/Native American, Multi-Racial, White

I am not represented by any of these options and want to recommend
another group.
The form adds: “As we define race, it can be easy to conflate
race with ethnicity or nationality because the definition and
boundaries are always changing. Use the US Census
(https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=59355&L=27619&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuY2Vuc3VzLmdvdi90b3BpY3MvcG9wdWxhdGlvbi9yYWNlL2Fib3V0Lmh0bWw/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=)
as a guide
but do not let it limit you based on how you personally identify
racially.

The form also asks if respondents are interested in new LGBTQIA+
“Affinity Spaces.” Those spaces are divided into “BIPOC
(Black/Indigenous/People of Color) LGBTQIA+” and “White
LGBTQIA+.”

A DCPS memo
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=59355&L=27620&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2RjLXB1YmxpYy1zY2hvb2xzLWFmZmluaXR5LXNwYWNlcy1hcHJpbC0yMDIyLXBncy04LTEwLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==]
details events to be held in June 2021 and is titled “Proposed
Engagement in Response to Recent Racial Incidents” begins: “These
are troubling times. I imagine that we all are struggling to make
sense of the murder of George Floyd and the continued racial violence
and racism that people of color, but black people specifically, endure
at the hands of the police, other systems and individuals.”

A table of proposed events in the memo includes an “Affinity Group
Brown Bag” that is described as a “Moderated space for CO [Central
Office] staff to reflect, connect, feel, share, strategize in smaller,
affinity (Black, White, Latinx, Asian) space: Focus on self-awareness,
identity and cultural awareness.”

An undated email from the DCPS Equity Team to AAPI Affinity (DCPS);
Black Affinity (DCPS); Hispanic/Latinx Affinity (DCPS); Multi-Racial
Affinity (DCPS); White Affinity (DCPS); and many individual DCPS
members with the subject line “Cross-Racial Affinity Space (led by
the Multi-Racial Affinity Group)” informs recipients that: “The
Multi-Racial Affinity group is tentative to lead a cross-racial
affinity space during the week of August 9th – August 13th
[2020]”:

Cross-Racial Affinity Space Schedule

The current schedule for cross-racial dialogue is as follows (open to
all affinity group members) to be led by respective affinity groups).
Dates may change if conflicts arise for a majority of attendees:

* October: To be led by the Hispanic/Latino/Latinx affinity group
* December: To be led by the White affinity group
* February: To be led by the Black affinity group
* May: To be led by the Asian American Pacific Islander (AAPI)
affinity group
*August: To be led by the Multi-Racial affinity group

***

A few guiding norms and goals for all affinity spaces:

* Go beyond celebration: Central Office (CO) affinity spaces will
ensure that the conversation translates identity-related issues into
action that helps mitigate those issues in our teams, offices and CO.
* Isolate Race: CO affinity spaces will leverage the Courageous
Conversation protocol
<https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=59355&L=27622&F=T&drurl=aHR0cDovL2llbC5vcmcvc2l0ZXMvZGVmYXVsdC9maWxlcy9HMTAtY291cmFnZW91cy1jb252ZXJzYXRpb25wcm90b2NvbC1vdmVydmlldy5wZGY/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=>
[no longer available on the IEL website] – especially the norm of
isolating race – in dialogue and collaboration.
* A lens for equity: As CO affinity spaces transition from
conversation into action, spaces will ensure those actions are rooted
in an equity lens that focuses on policy, identity and mindsets,
practices and culture. The DCPS Equity Framework
<https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=59355&L=27623&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly9kY3BzLmRjLmdvdi9zaXRlcy9kZWZhdWx0L2ZpbGVzL2RjL3NpdGVzL2RjcHMvcGFnZV9jb250ZW50L2F0dGFjaG1lbnRzL0RDUFMgRXF1aXR5IEZyYW1ld29ya18yMDE4LnBkZj91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=59355&L=27641&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly9kY3BzLmRjLmdvdi9zaXRlcy9kZWZhdWx0L2ZpbGVzL2RjL3NpdGVzL2RjcHMvcGFnZV9jb250ZW50L2F0dGFjaG1lbnRzL0RDUFNFcXVpdHlGcmFtZXdvcmtfMjAxOC5wZGY/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=]>
is a foundational resource for exploring goals and objectives through
an equity lens.
* Create Cross-Racial Learning Opportunities: CO affinity spaces
will come together in one space for interracial dialogue and learning
led by a respective affinity group every other month.

A January 26, 2021, email
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=59355&L=27624&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2RjLXB1YmxpYy1zY2hvb2xzLWFmZmluaXR5LXNwYWNlcy1hcHJpbC0yMDIyLXBncy00Ny01MC8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=]
from former DCPS Equity Strategy and Programming Team member Elizabeth
Rene
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=59355&L=27625&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubGlua2VkaW4uY29tL2luL2VsaXphYmV0aHJlbmUvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz],
who now works at
Google, introduces to her then-colleagues what is called the
“Anti-Racist Educator University,” which is touted as the first
such endeavor “led by any school district.” The email states:

Many of you have engaged in conversations about race and equity with
your students, families and colleagues. However, many more of you have
asked how to translate those conversations into action.

Anti-Racist Educator University is an opportunity to proactively apply
what we’ve learned about race and equity to our daily practice in
the classroom as well as shifting policies, mindsets and culture.

Anti-Racist Educator University is a strategic lever that provides
DCPS educators with shared learning rooted in a collective commitment
to active anti-racism...
A June 16, 2021, email
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=59355&L=27626&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2RjLXB1YmxpYy1zY2hvb2xzLWFmZmluaXR5LXNwYWNlcy1hcHJpbC0yMDIyLXBncy0xNTgtMTU5Lz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==]
from Samuel Cuadro
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=59355&L=27627&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubGlua2VkaW4uY29tL2luL3NhbXVlbC1jdWFkcm8tOGI1Mzk4MTNiLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==]
of DCPS to
Principal Katie Lundgren
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=59355&L=27628&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYnVzaGNlbnRlci5vcmcvcHVibGljYXRpb25zL2FydGljbGVzLzIwMTUvMDgvcHJpbmNpcGFsLWxlYWRlcnNoaXAtdGhlLWluc3RydWN0aXZlLWpvdXJuZXktb2Yta2F0aWUtbHVuZGdyZW4uaHRtbD91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==]
and several colleagues states: “[T]he goal of these affinity groups
is to create a safe space among colleagues to process the impacts of
racism and white supremacy within our school community and identify
collective actions to take as individuals and as groups.”

These shocking documents show, in evident violation of the
Constitution and civil rights laws, that the public school system of
our nation’s capital pushed blatant racial segregation among its
staff based on radical, divisive CRT principles.

Washington, DC is hardly alone.

We recently released records revealing critical race theory (CRT)
instruction at the U.S. Military Academy, West Point
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=59355&L=27536&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvY3J0LXByb3BhZ2FuZGEtYXQtd2VzdC1wb2ludC8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=].
One
training slide contains a graphic titled “MODERN-DAY SLAVERY IN THE
USA.”

Consumer Financial Protection Bureau records
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=59355&L=26852&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZmVkLWFnZW5jeS1zdXBwb3NlZC1taWNyb2FnZ3Jlc3Npb25zLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==]
we obtained in February 2022 included a PowerPoint presentation titled
“Race and gender based microaggressions” that was used for
training at the organization.

In June 2022, we appealed
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=59355&L=27446&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvanctYXBwZWFscy1jb3VydC1kZWNpc2lvbi1kaXNtaXNzaW5nLWxhd3N1aXQvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz]
a federal court decision dismissing a civil rights lawsuit on behalf
of David Flynn, a Massachusetts father who was fired from his position
as high school football coach after he raised concerns over Black
Lives Matter/critical race theory being taught in his daughter’s
seventh-grade ancient history class.

In January, we announced
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=59355&L=26556&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZmJpLWdhcmxhbmQtbWVtby8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=]
a FOIA lawsuit for
all FBI records related to the October 4, 2021, memorandum issued by
Attorney General Garland targeting parents who raised objections to
Critical Race Theory in schools.

In November 2021, we announced
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=59355&L=27519&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvbW9udGdvbWVyeS1zY2hvb2xzLWNydC8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=]
that we
received two sets
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=59355&L=26202&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL3RhZ3MvbW9udGdvbWVyeS1jby1zZXB0LW9jdC0yMDIxLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==]
of new records related to the teaching of critical race theory in
Montgomery County Public Schools (MCPS), Maryland’s largest school
system. The new records include a training course with information
about a book titled “Antiracist Baby” that introduces the youngest
readers to “the concept and power of antiracism,” and says it’s
the “perfect gift” for “ages baby to age 3.”

Records received from Loudoun County, VA
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=59355&L=26386&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvanctbG91ZG91bi1jb3VudHkvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz],
that we made
public in October 2021, revealed a coordinated effort to advance
critical race theory initiatives in Loudoun County public schools
despite widespread public opposition.

Also in October 2021, we received
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=59355&L=26395&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvanctZXhwb3Nlcy1jcnQtc2Nob29scy8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=]
a training
document from a whistleblower in the Westerly School District of Rhode
Island, which details how Westerly Public Schools are using teachers
to push critical race theory in classrooms.

Records from Wellesley Public Schools in Massachusetts, that we
released in June 2021, confirmed the use of “affinity spaces
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=59355&L=27630&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvY3J0LWFmZmluaXR5LXNwYWNlcy9d4oCdP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz
that divide
students and staff based on race as a priority and objective of the
school district’s “diversity, equity and inclusion” plan. The
school district also admitted
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=59355&L=25184&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL3dlbGxlc2xleS1hZmZpbml0eS1zcGFjZXMtanVuZS0yMDIxLXBnLTIvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz]
that between September 1, 2020 and May 17, 2021, it created “five
distinct” segregated spaces.

JUDICIAL WATCH SUES AFTER NEW YORK CITY FAILS TO CLEAN VOTER ROLLS

We sued New York after it failed to clean voter rolls. Federal law
requires local officials to make a reasonable effort to remove people
who have moved or died from voting rolls.

New York City failed to follow this law for years, so we filed a
federal lawsuit against New York State and New York City election
officials for failing to remove potentially hundreds of thousands of
ineligible voters from New York City voter registration.

Our lawsuit, filed under the National Voter Registration Act
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=59355&L=435&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVzdGljZS5nb3YvY3J0L25hdGlvbmFsLXZvdGVyLXJlZ2lzdHJhdGlvbi1hY3QtMTk5My1udnJhP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz]
(NVRA), details how New York City removed only 22 names under the
federal law over six years (_Judicial Watch v Valentine et al._
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=59355&L=27639&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL255LWJvYXJkLW9mLWVsZWN0aW9ucy1udnJhLWp1bHktMjAyMi1jb21wbGFpbnQtMDM5NTIvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz]
(No.1:22-cv-03952)).

The NVRA requires states to “conduct a general program that makes a
reasonable effort to remove” from the official voter rolls “the
names of ineligible voters” who have died or changed residence.
Among other things, the law requires registrations to be cancelled
when voters fail to respond to address confirmation notices and then
fail to vote in the next two general federal elections. In 2018, the
Supreme Court confirmed that such removals are mandatory (_Husted v.
A. Philip Randolph Inst.
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=59355&L=26309&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuc3VwcmVtZWNvdXJ0Lmdvdi9vcGluaW9ucy8xN3BkZi8xNi05ODBfZjJxMy5wZGZdXz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==
(138 S.
Ct. 1833, 1841-42 (2018)).

Our lawsuit details that New York City’s “own recent data concedes
that there were only 22 total” removals under this provision
“during a six-year period, in a city of over 5.5 million voters.
These are ludicrously small numbers of removals given the sizable
populations of these counties.” The lawsuit elaborates:

For context, the estimated number of voting-age citizens changing
residence, per year, during the five-year period from 2016 through
2020, in the five counties of New York City was:

about 194,000 in Kings County,
about 127,000 in Queens County,
about 190,000 in New York County,
about 82,000 in Bronx County, and
about 21,000 in Richmond County.
In all, “more than 600,000 voting-age citizens, _per year_, are
estimated to have changed residence in New York City during the
five-year period from 2016 through 2020.”

We note that “Yates County, one of the smallest counties in New
York, with a current total registration of about 14,500 voters” made
1,251 removals under this NVRA provision during the same six-year
period. “This is, literally, an exponentially greater number than
the 22 NVRA Removals made during the same period in all of New York
City.”

The lawsuit concludes that the “almost complete failure of Kings,
Queens, New York, Bronx, and Richmond Counties, over a period of at
least six years, to remove voters” under a key provision of federal
law “means that there are untold numbers of New York City
registrations for voters who are ineligible to vote at their listed
address because they have changed residence or are otherwise
ineligible to vote.”

Dirty voting rolls can mean dirty elections, and New York City’s
rolls are some of the dirtiest in the country. Elections officials in
New York City have simply refused to clean the voter lists for years.
We want cleaner elections, as the law requires, and we expect this
lawsuit will cause New York to take the simple steps necessary to
clean from its rolls the names of hundreds of thousands of voters who
have moved away or died.

We are a national leader in voting integrity and voting rights. As
part of this effort, we assembled a team of highly experienced voting
rights attorneys who stopped discriminatory elections in Hawaii, and
cleaned up voter rolls in California, Ohio, Indiana, and Kentucky,
among other achievements
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=59355&L=21208&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvdGFnL252cmEvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz].

California settled
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=59355&L=15011&F=T&]
an NVRA lawsuit with us and began the process of removing up to 1.6
million inactive names from Los Angeles County’s voter rolls.
Kentucky
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=59355&L=17111&F=T&]
also began a cleanup of hundreds of thousands of old registrations
last year after it entered into a consent decree to end another
Judicial Watch lawsuit.

In February 2022, we settled
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=59355&L=26811&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvbm9ydGgtY2Fyb2xpbmEtdm90ZXItcm9sbHMtbGF3c3VpdC8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=]
a
voter roll clean-up lawsuit against North Carolina and two of its
counties after the North Carolina removed over 430,000 ineligible
names from the voter rolls.

In March 2022, a Maryland court ruled in favor
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=59355&L=27012&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvanVkaWNpYWwtd2F0Y2gtdmljdG9yeS1jb3VydC1ydWxlcy1hZ2FpbnN0LWdlcnJ5bWFuZGVyLWluLW1hcnlsYW5kLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==]
of our challenge to Maryland’s Democratic legislature “extreme”
congressional redistricting gerrymander.

In May 2022, we sued
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=59355&L=27407&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvbGF3c3VpdC1hZ2FpbnN0LWlsbGlub2lzLXRvLXN0b3AtY291bnRpbmctb2YtYmFsbG90cy1hZnRlci1lbGVjdGlvbi1kYXkvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz]
Illinois on behalf of Congressman Mike Bost and two other registered
Illinois voters to prevent state election officials from extending
Election Day for 14 days beyond the date established by federal law.

$195 MILLION TO ‘REDRESS THE LEGACY OF HARM’ IN RACIST
TRANSPORTATION INFRASTRUCTURE

You may be interested to know that highways can be racist, and the
Biden administration will hand out millions to grant applicants
seeking “equity and environmental justice” to fix or even
dismantle them! Our _Corruption Chronicles_ blog details
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=59355&L=27635&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvcmFjaXN0LXRyYW5zcG9ydGF0aW9uLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==]
this
ideological crusade against transportation infrastructure.

In the Biden administration’s latest racial equity project, American
taxpayers will spend $195 million to help connect minority communities
that are cut off from economic opportunities by racist transportation
infrastructure. The costly plan is known as Reconnecting Communities
Pilot (RCP) and it is part of the Department of Transportation’s
(DOT) “Equity Strategy Goal to reduce inequities” across the
nation’s transportation systems and the communities they effect. In
its announcement
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=59355&L=27642&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudHJhbnNwb3J0YXRpb24uZ292L2JyaWVmaW5nLXJvb20vYmlkZW4tYWRtaW5pc3RyYXRpb24tYW5ub3VuY2VzLWZpcnN0LWV2ZXItZnVuZGluZy1wcm9ncmFtLWRlZGljYXRlZC1yZWNvbm5lY3Rpbmc/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=],
the DOT writes that “preference will be given to applications from
economically disadvantaged communities, especially those with projects
that are focused on equity and environmental justice, have strong
community engagement and stewardship, and a commitment to shared
prosperity and equitable development.” The language sounds like
material found in a communist manifesto.

DOT Secretary Pete Buttigieg justifies the investment by explaining
that “transportation can connect us to jobs, services, and loved
ones, but we ‘ve also seen countless cases around the country where
a piece of infrastructure cuts off a neighborhood or a community
because of how it was built.” RCP is the first-ever initiative
funded by the federal government that is completely dedicated to
unifying neighborhoods living with the impacts of infrastructure that
divides them, Buttigieg adds. It will help reconnect communities that
are cut off from economic opportunities by what the administration
seems to claim is a racist transportation infrastructure. In fact, the
lengthy grant announcement
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=59355&L=27643&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudHJhbnNwb3J0YXRpb24uZ292L3NpdGVzL2RvdC5nb3YvZmlsZXMvMjAyMi0wNi9SQ1BfTk9GT19GWTIyLnBkZj91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==]
states that the multi-million-dollar community reconnection program
“seeks to redress the legacy of harm caused by transportation
infrastructure.” The “harm” includes barriers to opportunity,
displacement, damage to the environment and public health, limited
access and “other hardships,” according to the document.

In pursuit of redressing the legacy of harm, RCP “will support and
engage economically disadvantaged communities to increase affordable,
accessible, and multimodal access to daily destinations like jobs,
healthcare, grocery stores, schools, places of worship, recreation,
and park space,” the administration writes in the grant
announcement. Thus, the new program will be implemented in line with a
multitude of other federal initiatives launched by a 2021 Biden
executive order
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=59355&L=27254&F=T&]
to advance racial equity and support for underserved communities
through the federal government. Besides the DOT’s Equity Action
Plan, the agency grant document identifies them as federal actions to
address environmental justice in minority and low-income populations,
affordable housing in the nation’s most desirable neighborhoods and
a program to strengthen the economy through the creation of
good-paying jobs with the free and fair choice to join a union, strong
labor standards, and workforce programs. There are many more that were
left out of the RCP document.

In the last year, key federal agencies have implemented racial equity
plans as per Biden’s order. The Department of Justice (DOJ) created
a special initiative to advance equity for marginalized and
underserved communities. The Department of Labor (DOL) dedicated $260
million to promote “equitable access” to government unemployment
benefits by addressing disparities in the administration and delivery
of money by race ethnicity and language proficiency. The Treasury
Department named its first ever racial equity chief, a veteran La Raza
official who spent a decade at the nation’s most influential open
borders group. The Department of Defense (DOD) is using outrageous
anti-bias materials that indoctrinate troops with anti-American and
racially inflammatory training on diversity topics. The U. S.
Department of Agriculture (USDA) created an equity commission to
address longstanding inequities in agriculture. The nation’s medical
research agency has a special minority health and health disparities
division that recently issued a study declaring COVID-19 exacerbated
preexisting resentment against racial/ethnic minorities and
marginalized communities. The Transportation Security Administration
(TSA) recently hired a Chief Diversity and Inclusion Officer
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=59355&L=27418&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvdHNhLWRpdmVyc2l0eS1pbmNsdXNpb24vP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz]
even though
most of its employees come from “underrepresented racial and ethnic
groups.” Just a few days ago Judicial Watch reported
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=59355&L=27594&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvcmFjaWFsLWVxdWl0eS1pbi1mb29kLXN0YW1wLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==]
that the
administration is spending $6 million to advance racial equity in the
government’s food-stamp program that already serves a large minority
population.
Until next week...

clean_body: CRT Abuse in DC Schools! [INSIDE JW] DC Schools Pushed Racial Segregation in Employee ‘Affinity Spaces’ [] The school district in your nation’s capital is spending tax dollars to promote segregation based upon race and sexual identity. We received 194 pages [==] of records from District of Columbia Public Schools (DCPS) which show that DC officials pushed segregated “Affinity Spaces” on the basis of race and sexual identity. We obtained the records in response to a June 24, 2021, FOIA request for: Records identifying the number of affinity spaces hosted by District of Columbia Public Schools from August 31, 2020 to June 24, 2021. Records identifying the topics discussed during any affinity spaces hosted by District of Columbia Public Schools from August 31, 2020 to June 24, 2021. Records inviting students, faculty, and staff to affinity spaces hosted by District of Columbia Public Schools from August 31, 2020 to June 24, 2021. Records, including policies and procedures, regarding the creation and use of “affinity spaces.” Any analyses of whether affinity spaces excluding students, faculty, and staff who identify as a specific race or gender is consistent with district and federal law, including but not limited to 42 U.S.C. 2000d and the Equal Protection clause of the 14th Amendment of the U.S. Constitution. The records include a DCPS September 2021 PowerPoint presentation titled “DCPS Affinity Group Interest Form [== stating: DCPS staff: The Equity Strategy and Programming Team initially launched affinity spaces as safe spaces for you to reflect and process following the murder of George Floyd, and we are going to continue them throughout the 21-22 school year as a place for folks to reflect and continue to learn and grow. One way to process in a safe space is through affinity. Affinity spaces are gathering opportunities for people who share a common identity. This space will be organized based on the racial identities represented in Central Office as we aim to lean into the Courageous Conversation condition of isolating race. For more information about the benefit of racial affinity groups, please leverage this Learning for Justice resource: = DCPS Central Office staff from the Equity, Community Action and SEL [Social and Emotional Learning] Teams will co-facilitate these affinity groups in collaboration with volunteers at least once a month but more frequently as requested by the group. A form in the presentation asks respondents to submit their pronouns, which include she/her, she/they, he/him, he/they, they/them, ze/hir, she/he/they, or “other.” DCPS staff is asked to select “Which racial affinity group(s) do you plan to join via Teams? (Select all that apply to you and your racial identities. A separate calendar/Teams invite will come from the DCPS Equity calendar.):” Asian American Pacific Islander Black/African American, Hispanic/Latinx Indigenous/Native American, Multi-Racial, White I am not represented by any of these options and want to recommend another group. The form adds: “As we define race, it can be easy to conflate race with ethnicity or nationality because the definition and boundaries are always changing. Use the US Census (=) as a guide but do not let it limit you based on how you personally identify racially. The form also asks if respondents are interested in new LGBTQIA+ “Affinity Spaces.” Those spaces are divided into “BIPOC (Black/Indigenous/People of Color) LGBTQIA+” and “White LGBTQIA+.” A DCPS memo [==] details events to be held in June 2021 and is titled “Proposed Engagement in Response to Recent Racial Incidents” begins: “These are troubling times. I imagine that we all are struggling to make sense of the murder of George Floyd and the continued racial violence and racism that people of color, but black people specifically, endure at the hands of the police, other systems and individuals.” A table of proposed events in the memo includes an “Affinity Group Brown Bag” that is described as a “Moderated space for CO [Central Office] staff to reflect, connect, feel, share, strategize in smaller, affinity (Black, White, Latinx, Asian) space: Focus on self-awareness, identity and cultural awareness.” An undated email from the DCPS Equity Team to AAPI Affinity (DCPS); Black Affinity (DCPS); Hispanic/Latinx Affinity (DCPS); Multi-Racial Affinity (DCPS); White Affinity (DCPS); and many individual DCPS members with the subject line “Cross-Racial Affinity Space (led by the Multi-Racial Affinity Group)” informs recipients that: “The Multi-Racial Affinity group is tentative to lead a cross-racial affinity space during the week of August 9th – August 13th [2020]”: Cross-Racial Affinity Space Schedule The current schedule for cross-racial dialogue is as follows (open to all affinity group members) to be led by respective affinity groups). Dates may change if conflicts arise for a majority of attendees: October: To be led by the Hispanic/Latino/Latinx affinity group December: To be led by the White affinity group February: To be led by the Black affinity group May: To be led by the Asian American Pacific Islander (AAPI) affinity group August: To be led by the Multi-Racial affinity group A few guiding norms and goals for all affinity spaces: Go beyond celebration: Central Office (CO) affinity spaces will ensure that the conversation translates identity-related issues into action that helps mitigate those issues in our teams, offices and CO. Isolate Race: CO affinity spaces will leverage the Courageous Conversation protocol <=> [no longer available on the IEL website] – especially the norm of isolating race – in dialogue and collaboration. A lens for equity: As CO affinity spaces transition from conversation into action, spaces will ensure those actions are rooted in an equity lens that focuses on policy, identity and mindsets, practices and culture. The DCPS Equity Framework <== [=]> is a foundational resource for exploring goals and objectives through an equity lens. * Create Cross-Racial Learning Opportunities: CO affinity spaces will come together in one space for interracial dialogue and learning led by a respective affinity group every other month. A January 26, 2021, email [=] from former DCPS Equity Strategy and Programming Team member Elizabeth Rene [], who now works at Google, introduces to her then-colleagues what is called the “Anti-Racist Educator University,” which is touted as the first such endeavor “led by any school district.” The email states: Many of you have engaged in conversations about race and equity with your students, families and colleagues. However, many more of you have asked how to translate those conversations into action. Anti-Racist Educator University is an opportunity to proactively apply what we’ve learned about race and equity to our daily practice in the classroom as well as shifting policies, mindsets and culture. Anti-Racist Educator University is a strategic lever that provides DCPS educators with shared learning rooted in a collective commitment to active anti-racism... A June 16, 2021, email [==] from Samuel Cuadro [==] of DCPS to Principal Katie Lundgren [==] and several colleagues states: “[T]he goal of these affinity groups is to create a safe space among colleagues to process the impacts of racism and white supremacy within our school community and identify collective actions to take as individuals and as groups.” These shocking documents show, in evident violation of the Constitution and civil rights laws, that the public school system of our nation’s capital pushed blatant racial segregation among its staff based on radical, divisive CRT principles. Washington, DC is hardly alone. We recently released records revealing critical race theory (CRT) instruction at the U.S. Military Academy, West Point [=]. One training slide contains a graphic titled “MODERN-DAY SLAVERY IN THE USA.” Consumer Financial Protection Bureau records [==] we obtained in February 2022 included a PowerPoint presentation titled “Race and gender based microaggressions” that was used for training at the organization. In June 2022, we appealed [] a federal court decision dismissing a civil rights lawsuit on behalf of David Flynn, a Massachusetts father who was fired from his position as high school football coach after he raised concerns over Black Lives Matter/critical race theory being taught in his daughter’s seventh-grade ancient history class. In January, we announced [=] a FOIA lawsuit for all FBI records related to the October 4, 2021, memorandum issued by Attorney General Garland targeting parents who raised objections to Critical Race Theory in schools. In November 2021, we announced [=] that we received two sets [==] of new records related to the teaching of critical race theory in Montgomery County Public Schools (MCPS), Maryland’s largest school system. The new records include a training course with information about a book titled “Antiracist Baby” that introduces the youngest readers to “the concept and power of antiracism,” and says it’s the “perfect gift” for “ages baby to age 3.” Records received from Loudoun County, VA [], that we made public in October 2021, revealed a coordinated effort to advance critical race theory initiatives in Loudoun County public schools despite widespread public opposition. Also in October 2021, we received [=] a training document from a whistleblower in the Westerly School District of Rhode Island, which details how Westerly Public Schools are using teachers to push critical race theory in classrooms. Records from Wellesley Public Schools in Massachusetts, that we released in June 2021, confirmed the use of “affinity spaces [ that divide students and staff based on race as a priority and objective of the school district’s “diversity, equity and inclusion” plan. The school district also admitted [] that between September 1, 2020 and May 17, 2021, it created “five distinct” segregated spaces. JUDICIAL WATCH SUES AFTER NEW YORK CITY FAILS TO CLEAN VOTER ROLLS We sued New York after it failed to clean voter rolls. Federal law requires local officials to make a reasonable effort to remove people who have moved or died from voting rolls. New York City failed to follow this law for years, so we filed a federal lawsuit against New York State and New York City election officials for failing to remove potentially hundreds of thousands of ineligible voters from New York City voter registration. Our lawsuit, filed under the National Voter Registration Act [] (NVRA), details how New York City removed only 22 names under the federal law over six years (Judicial Watch v Valentine et al. [] (No.1:22-cv-03952)). The NVRA requires states to “conduct a general program that makes a reasonable effort to remove” from the official voter rolls “the names of ineligible voters” who have died or changed residence. Among other things, the law requires registrations to be cancelled when voters fail to respond to address confirmation notices and then fail to vote in the next two general federal elections. In 2018, the Supreme Court confirmed that such removals are mandatory (Husted v. A. Philip Randolph Inst. [== (138 S. Ct. 1833, 1841-42 (2018)). Our lawsuit details that New York City’s “own recent data concedes that there were only 22 total” removals under this provision “during a six-year period, in a city of over 5.5 million voters. These are ludicrously small numbers of removals given the sizable populations of these counties.” The lawsuit elaborates: For context, the estimated number of voting-age citizens changing residence, per year, during the five-year period from 2016 through 2020, in the five counties of New York City was: about 194,000 in Kings County, about 127,000 in Queens County, about 190,000 in New York County, about 82,000 in Bronx County, and about 21,000 in Richmond County. In all, “more than 600,000 voting-age citizens, per year, are estimated to have changed residence in New York City during the five-year period from 2016 through 2020.” We note that “Yates County, one of the smallest counties in New York, with a current total registration of about 14,500 voters” made 1,251 removals under this NVRA provision during the same six-year period. “This is, literally, an exponentially greater number than the 22 NVRA Removals made during the same period in all of New York City.” The lawsuit concludes that the “almost complete failure of Kings, Queens, New York, Bronx, and Richmond Counties, over a period of at least six years, to remove voters” under a key provision of federal law “means that there are untold numbers of New York City registrations for voters who are ineligible to vote at their listed address because they have changed residence or are otherwise ineligible to vote.” Dirty voting rolls can mean dirty elections, and New York City’s rolls are some of the dirtiest in the country. Elections officials in New York City have simply refused to clean the voter lists for years. We want cleaner elections, as the law requires, and we expect this lawsuit will cause New York to take the simple steps necessary to clean from its rolls the names of hundreds of thousands of voters who have moved away or died. We are a national leader in voting integrity and voting rights. As part of this effort, we assembled a team of highly experienced voting rights attorneys who stopped discriminatory elections in Hawaii, and cleaned up voter rolls in California, Ohio, Indiana, and Kentucky, among other achievements []. California settled [=] an NVRA lawsuit with us and began the process of removing up to 1.6 million inactive names from Los Angeles County’s voter rolls. Kentucky [==] also began a cleanup of hundreds of thousands of old registrations last year after it entered into a consent decree to end another Judicial Watch lawsuit. In February 2022, we settled [=] a voter roll clean-up lawsuit against North Carolina and two of its counties after the North Carolina removed over 430,000 ineligible names from the voter rolls. In March 2022, a Maryland court ruled in favor [==] of our challenge to Maryland’s Democratic legislature “extreme” congressional redistricting gerrymander. In May 2022, we sued [] Illinois on behalf of Congressman Mike Bost and two other registered Illinois voters to prevent state election officials from extending Election Day for 14 days beyond the date established by federal law. $195 MILLION TO ‘REDRESS THE LEGACY OF HARM’ IN RACIST TRANSPORTATION INFRASTRUCTURE You may be interested to know that highways can be racist, and the Biden administration will hand out millions to grant applicants seeking “equity and environmental justice” to fix or even dismantle them! Our Corruption Chronicles blog details [==] this ideological crusade against transportation infrastructure. In the Biden administration’s latest racial equity project, American taxpayers will spend $195 million to help connect minority communities that are cut off from economic opportunities by racist transportation infrastructure. The costly plan is known as Reconnecting Communities Pilot (RCP) and it is part of the Department of Transportation’s (DOT) “Equity Strategy Goal to reduce inequities” across the nation’s transportation systems and the communities they effect. In its announcement [=], the DOT writes that “preference will be given to applications from economically disadvantaged communities, especially those with projects that are focused on equity and environmental justice, have strong community engagement and stewardship, and a commitment to shared prosperity and equitable development.” The language sounds like material found in a communist manifesto. DOT Secretary Pete Buttigieg justifies the investment by explaining that “transportation can connect us to jobs, services, and loved ones, but we ‘ve also seen countless cases around the country where a piece of infrastructure cuts off a neighborhood or a community because of how it was built.” RCP is the first-ever initiative funded by the federal government that is completely dedicated to unifying neighborhoods living with the impacts of infrastructure that divides them, Buttigieg adds. It will help reconnect communities that are cut off from economic opportunities by what the administration seems to claim is a racist transportation infrastructure. In fact, the lengthy grant announcement [==] states that the multi-million-dollar community reconnection program “seeks to redress the legacy of harm caused by transportation infrastructure.” The “harm” includes barriers to opportunity, displacement, damage to the environment and public health, limited access and “other hardships,” according to the document. In pursuit of redressing the legacy of harm, RCP “will support and engage economically disadvantaged communities to increase affordable, accessible, and multimodal access to daily destinations like jobs, healthcare, grocery stores, schools, places of worship, recreation, and park space,” the administration writes in the grant announcement. Thus, the new program will be implemented in line with a multitude of other federal initiatives launched by a 2021 Biden executive order [==] to advance racial equity and support for underserved communities through the federal government. Besides the DOT’s Equity Action Plan, the agency grant document identifies them as federal actions to address environmental justice in minority and low-income populations, affordable housing in the nation’s most desirable neighborhoods and a program to strengthen the economy through the creation of good-paying jobs with the free and fair choice to join a union, strong labor standards, and workforce programs. There are many more that were left out of the RCP document. In the last year, key federal agencies have implemented racial equity plans as per Biden’s order. The Department of Justice (DOJ) created a special initiative to advance equity for marginalized and underserved communities. The Department of Labor (DOL) dedicated $260 million to promote “equitable access” to government unemployment benefits by addressing disparities in the administration and delivery of money by race ethnicity and language proficiency. The Treasury Department named its first ever racial equity chief, a veteran La Raza official who spent a decade at the nation’s most influential open borders group. The Department of Defense (DOD) is using outrageous anti-bias materials that indoctrinate troops with anti-American and racially inflammatory training on diversity topics. The U. S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) created an equity commission to address longstanding inequities in agriculture. The nation’s medical research agency has a special minority health and health disparities division that recently issued a study declaring COVID-19 exacerbated preexisting resentment against racial/ethnic minorities and marginalized communities. The Transportation Security Administration (TSA) recently hired a Chief Diversity and Inclusion Officer [] even though most of its employees come from “underrepresented racial and ethnic groups.” Just a few days ago Judicial Watch reported [==] that the administration is spending $6 million to advance racial equity in the government’s food-stamp program that already serves a large minority population. Until next week... [Contribute] [] [32x32x1] [] [32x32x2] [] [32x32x3] [] [32x32x3] [==] Judicial Watch, Inc. 425 3rd St Sw Ste 800 Washington, DC 20024 202.646.5172 © 2017 - 2022, All Rights Reserved Manage Email Subscriptions [] |

subject: Ben & Jerry's Anti-Isreal Boycott
body:
On the Hempstead Town Council, I was extremely proud to pass one of the nation’s first anti-BDS laws and to take action against Ben & Jerry’s for their discriminatory conduct.

Peter,

The decision by Ben & Jerry’s parent company, Unilever, to end the ice cream maker’s discriminatory boycott of Israel is a big win for those of us who have stood strong against the anti-Israel Boycott, Divestment and Sanctions movement.

On the Hempstead Town Council, I was extremely proud to pass one of the nation’s first anti-BDS laws and to take action against Ben & Jerry’s for their discriminatory conduct.

Israel is our most important ally and we can never let those succeed who wish to isolate Israel and do it harm.

name: Bryan Steil, Congressman
subject: Election Integrity Update: WI Supreme Court Decision
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY STEIL FOR WISCONSIN, INC
body:
The Wisconsin Supreme Court today ruled that unmanned drop boxes are not in accordance with state law. However, Nancy Pelosi and Joe Biden are still pushing legislation that would nationalize elections and supersede voting laws on the books in stat
Hi Peter,The Wisconsin Supreme Court today ruled that unmanned drop boxes are not in accordance with state law._**However, Nancy Pelosi and Joe Biden are still pushing legislation that would nationalize elections and supersede voting laws on the books in states, including Wisconsin.**_ Their dream bill would allow for ballot harvesting across the country, and important voting safeguards like Voter ID would be gutted. The far left wants to impose California and New York-style elections nationwide.**We cannot allow this to happen.** I've been leading the charge against it in Congress, but I need **YOUR** help.[**If you support measures that will safeguard our elections, please consider donating today so we can continue to bolster our resources for November and create a "Red Wave" that ends the Democrats' one-party rule in Washington.**](https://email.rally.digital/t/j-l-vyhdtit-idtjvlhlh-r/)Thank you,Bryan
--
**Bryan Steil**
Congressman [Help Us Fight Back!](https://email.rally.digital/t/j-l-vyhdtit-idtjvlhlh-y/) [](https://email.rally.digital/t/j-l-vyhdtit-idtjvlhlh-j/)[](https://email.rally.digital/t/j-l-vyhdtit-idtjvlhlh-t/)[](https://email.rally.digital/t/j-l-vyhdtit-idtjvlhlh-i/) [ Forward ](https://steilforwisconsininc.forwardtomyfriend.com/j-idtjvlhlh-C89B6A88-vyhdtit-l-h) | PAID FOR BY STEIL FOR WISCONSIN, INC

name: Lawrence W. Reed
subject: A Gallon of Gas Now Costs More Than the Federal Minimum Wage in These U.S. Cities
body:
Inflation is hurting the working and middle class the most.
[View this message in your browser](http://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2zcs783hf442mou52tklqsb8gmorbtm9r54smffs8_rp2ash2s8gb2sbgebjqf99jk5p7atdge5p78d5jmeqjetjjkorrbdt469r31d5jekrq9onicbrbctjnmlhbeobhmdgb8dj5c4ob3dhjctgbddlleqrrfddjcaq76t3jmmo65cbnmv970sj46oob4pjiegonco70mhij1o5l65iecpj666pbbdhnm9o6cclgmdiefc5lclp238h1e8he4933cljmcor0cjh69oh5u)
[](http://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2zcrm5embqjskrs60eg5nlj19tcau79j2pbjccov0_rp22sh2s8i66pb7cphm0p72cjh2bs)

Friend,

Inflation is hurting the working and middle class the most.

And the only advice we get from politicians and celebrities to save on gas is to buy an $80,000 Tesla. Government policies always have consequences. But the last two years of Biden's policies have been particularly disastrous.

For the first time ever, gas prices rose above $4 in every state. In California, the average price per gallon was $6.06 as of Monday. **In some parts of the Golden State, however, prices reached $7.83!**

(By the time you receive this email, gas prices will likely be even higher).

Do NOT let the media or the Biden administration fool you. Inflation is directly caused by the government printing too much money.

The problem is most people, especially high school students, don't have a solid understanding of economics. So these lies by politicians go unquestioned.

**Many people don 't understand that inflation is caused by the money supply expanding, and the government's policies are responsible.**

I am on a personal quest to teach MILLIONS of high school students about free market economics through the Foundation for Economic Education.

[_**Friend, can you help a teenager learn about the importance of free markets, entrepreneurship, and limited government? Click here to donate $5, $10, $50, or any amount today >>>**_](http://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2zvihin52c4ri45vbqpm82bnu84o9oqhf9203gg78_rp22sh2s8i66pb7cphm0p72cjh2bs)

I'm Lawrence Reed, President Emeritus for FEE, and I'd like to tell you exactly what FEE is doing to help students understand the current inflation crisis.

_FEE in the Classroom_ is a one-of-a-kind educational workshop that engages, inspires, and equips high school and college students with a deeper understanding of the principles of free enterprise, limited government, and entrepreneurship.

This unique program is held in both brick-and-mortar and virtual classrooms across the country.

Thousands of young Americans have come to understand the positive impact freedom has on their lives, their communities, and our country through FEE in the Classroom workshops.

One of the most frequently asked questions afterward is: _" Why haven't I heard these ideas before?"_

_**And FEE in the Classroom works. Our students know more about inflation than Biden does, Friend**_**. **

**I have a goal to teach over 23,000 students this year in FEE in the Classroom. **

[_**Can you donate $3, $23, $50, or any amount to FEE in the Classroom? Click here to send your donation >>>**_](http://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2z8ts8gla0in3pp1689tq7ianrgaspbaeof2i399o_rp22sh2s8i66pb7cphm0p72cjh2bs)

Our mission is to inspire, educate, and connect future leaders with the economic, ethical, and legal principles of a free society.

One of the ways we do this is by making the works of liberty's greatest thinkers available to students.

Let me show you some of FEE's amazing work just recently. **A few weeks ago, we sent 75,000 books to Nevada students.**

* 25,000 copies of I, Pencil, a short book about the complexities of making something as simple as a pencil, can only be achieved in the free market.
* 25,000 copies of Are We Rome?, a book about the fall of the Roman Empire.
* 25,000 copies of Myths of the Great Depression, which debunks myths on government intervention solving the issue and other economic myths.

[](http://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2zrf58s89mcserrcis659th4m63opmk0sm3meim2g_rp22sh2s8i66pb7cphm0p72cjh2bs)

**Friend, can you help a student learn about the importance of economic freedom today? Click any button below to donate.**

clean_body: Inflation is hurting the working and middle class the most. Friend, Inflation is hurting the working and middle class the most. And the only advice we get from politicians and celebrities to save on gas is to buy an $80,000 Tesla. Government policies always have consequences. But the last two years of Biden's policies have been particularly disastrous. For the first time ever, gas prices rose above $4 in every state. In California, the average price per gallon was $6.06 as of Monday. In some parts of the Golden State, however, prices reached $7.83! (By the time you receive this email, gas prices will likely be even higher). Do NOT let the media or the Biden administration fool you. Inflation is directly caused by the government printing too much money. The problem is most people, especially high school students, don't have a solid understanding of economics. So these lies by politicians go unquestioned. Many people don 't understand that inflation is caused by the money supply expanding, and the government's policies are responsible. I am on a personal quest to teach MILLIONS of high school students about free market economics through the Foundation for Economic Education. I'm Lawrence Reed, President Emeritus for FEE, and I'd like to tell you exactly what FEE is doing to help students understand the current inflation crisis. FEE in the Classroom is a one-of-a-kind educational workshop that engages, inspires, and equips high school and college students with a deeper understanding of the principles of free enterprise, limited government, and entrepreneurship. This unique program is held in both brick-and-mortar and virtual classrooms across the country. Thousands of young Americans have come to understand the positive impact freedom has on their lives, their communities, and our country through FEE in the Classroom workshops. One of the most frequently asked questions afterward is: " Why haven't I heard these ideas before?" And FEE in the Classroom works. Our students know more about inflation than Biden does, Friend. I have a goal to teach over 23,000 students this year in FEE in the Classroom. Our mission is to inspire, educate, and connect future leaders with the economic, ethical, and legal principles of a free society. One of the ways we do this is by making the works of liberty's greatest thinkers available to students. Let me show you some of FEE's amazing work just recently. A few weeks ago, we sent 75,000 books to Nevada students. 25,000 copies of I, Pencil, a short book about the complexities of making something as simple as a pencil, can only be achieved in the free market. 25,000 copies of Are We Rome?, a book about the fall of the Roman Empire. 25,000 copies of Myths of the Great Depression, which debunks myths on government intervention solving the issue and other economic myths. Friend, can you help a student learn about the importance of economic freedom today? Click any button below to donate. Thank you for helping the next generation gain a solid understanding of economics. Lawrence W. Reed President Emeritus, FEE P.S. FEE has been home to some of liberty's greatest thinkers, and our legacy includes the landmark works of writers who dared to go against the popularity of socialism during their time: Leonard Read's I, Pencil Henry Hazlitt's Economics in One Lesson Ludwig von Mises's Human Action * Translated and republished Frederic Bastiat's The Law FEE has been battling the ideas of socialism and communism in the classroom since 1947. Your urgent donation helps FEE continue that fight today. name: Brent Bozell
subject: What’s the difference between President Trump and the Taliban?
body:
A recently leaked chat between Twitter employees shows they were gleeful to ban President Trump and Twitter activists are now ta
[View this message in your browser](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2zqm24le3qll0tqqk60a99rtqj2d4un6llk8befso_rp2ash2s8gb2sbgebjqf99jk4qj4ttkumqn4s9nmorb79hnmmrq8cjm62qb6t9nkjhf4onlmlgmcsbh6mrj2p9keaq7cp70mep70o75elhr2tj6m4rj0p9nm3im2sjl66r61puie1p64pjgm9j71p5jcdg74dr661obbc37mti6cc9gm9j61o5lcdge1d9h66qj3dbi46h22sh2s8i66pb7cphm0o71cdh2bs)
[](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2zif014qa65vd9dss0foldkjrca9pv7nplcbrbp2o_rp22sh2s8i66pb7cphm0o71cdh2bs)

Friend,

President Trump is banned from Twitter, but the Taliban isn't. . .

Make no mistake--this is on purpose.

A recently leaked chat between Twitter employees shows they were gleeful to ban President Trump and Twitter activists are now taking aim at the powerful conservative Twitter exposing radical trans teachers for brainwashing children.

[](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2zid46ce3mggk2tba931hetkfpu20bercii8roie0_rp22sh2s8i66pb7cphm0o71cdh2bs)

We have to defeat these radical commie activists and I have a plan to do so.

**Let me introduce myself. My name is Brent Bozell, and I am the Founder and President of the Media Research Center (MRC).** MRC is the only conservative organization dedicated to exposing the radical left-wing agenda in the media.

MRC is fighting to expose media corruption--including Big Tech--and hold them accountable for their corruption and lies.

[_**Can you join the fight to hold Twitter accountable for its blatant corruption and to fight for Free Speech? Click here to send your donation of $25, $50, $100, or any amount >>>**_](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2zpg5dnl9eo4880qdabdjcpuk23rco1kd7vne358g_rp22sh2s8i66pb7cphm0o71cdh2bs)

Elon Musk recently bought Twitter, and he's fighting to protect Free Speech on the platform.

But he is inheriting a Big Tech machine designed to censor and suppress conservative voices such as the Media Research Center, the Babylon Bee, President Trump, and more.

**When the MRC was founded in 1987, over 75% of Americans trusted the left-wing media. Since then, MRC has successfully SHATTERED America 's trust in the left-wing media--over 90% of Americans state they DO NOT trust the media anymore. **

[](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2zm1h8ijuei26uud3pckndvbltd8qo6np9jkemu2o_rp22sh2s8i66pb7cphm0o71cdh2bs)

Here is how the Media Research Center exposes the left-wing media:

**FIRST:** The MRC monitors and clips videos of all content from major networks, social media accounts, and news outlets. In fact, they have over 800,000 hours of video stored on secure servers covering every nightly broadcast and more going back to 1987.

**SECOND:** My team and I analyze this content and identify fake news stories, lies, and media bias.

**THIRD:** MRC's team exposes these lies by forcing the media to report the story, sharing their reports on social media, and telling the truth. The Media Research Center also owns some of the largest social media pages in the conservative movement so that they can share these stories with supporters.

**MRC will expose Media and Big Tech corruption and hold them accountable.**

**Click any link below to donate. **
[_**Donate $25**_](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2zlbm2hgd80sce2t2kufvu8lpi9keoc94tgfkq45g_rp22sh2s8i66pb7cphm0o71cdh2bs)

[_**Donate $500**_](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2z6324d7gjlik4o5iot3mibr2rv4i3527bo9bibc0_rp22sh2s8i66pb7cphm0o71cdh2bs)
[_**Donate $1,000**_](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2z5kk80v9c795mbh3f5vqcktkelhg8ftrpfmm7i0g_rp22sh2s8i66pb7cphm0o71cdh2bs)
[_**Donate $2,500**_](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2z1p9d0f36foksiocsnu8g14p4v3gp77cb3ofcqn0_rp22sh2s8i66pb7cphm0o71cdh2bs)

body:
As a result of this crisis, survival food is more important than ever. [unsubscribe](http://track.americanpatriotdaily.com/?xul=6zs1j7skt6bmn2rn3be8ia2g8isc4z2aq9qw6lmxrpuf7g5fhbjq1ij70zo5ojdpa9ggdh1lnezqhb0jmw4twg22ihayshdhur2d52lfurn2c3nx94&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh)
---
Please read the message below from our sponsor, 4Patriots.

Steve Thompson, Editor

* * *

|
# Outrage Over Survival Food
According to leading industry sources, grocery stores across the United States are worried about food shortages. Experts say more grocery hoarding may come as disruptions push America's food supply "near its breaking point". **As a result of this crisis, survival food is more important than ever.** If you don't take action or if you stockpile the wrong foods, you could be setting your family up to be hungry in a time of crisis. It sounds harsh, but the truth is too many people with good intentions are making critical mistakes with their survival food. Mistakes like...
* Getting MREs with a 5-year shelf life – depending on where you purchase them from they could be near expired...
* Getting gross survival foods that are tough to stomach and so high in salt, MSG and preservatives you could clog your arteries and get yourself sick...
* Or simply getting the wrong foods and leaving a critical hole in your meal plan, which means you risk becoming malnourished...

Well, no need to worry anymore. Obviously, waiting for the government to give me a handout in a disaster just wasn't an option for me. And I was completely turned off by the crazy high cost of survival food sold by some companies. That's why I ordered and got my very own stockpile of the best-selling [**4Patriots Survival Food Kits**](http://track.americanpatriotdaily.com/?xtl=juvuqb2ub0vx1n0je99jwivnalwfwcblfadis0zq3d6298hhck61edtec9c9giuzt5r7s9fw06msiuzepr863miskwywnidfikyc2j0fdhzkfpa5n9rfbrz8bwtfs97hwvsttmi42ch6odovudkt3soxwsfamrfdi3bpceoabwubuadq8i9s57we7ri9yn9bwssfbm42h84foz7bi9b1cluzcgzsadqvj26hpvd757gvo9wwyrlk1zm1osl5bc5qh5d51kriw59ted1g7af7&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh). Currently 4Patriots Survival Food Kits are flying off the shelves because:
* **4Patriots Survival Food Kits are a tremendous value.** This is _not_ ordinary food. This is delicious, nutritious, good-for-25-years super survival food that helps keep you from going hungry in a crisis. This is high-quality survival food without gross fillers or weird "frankenfood" that the other guys use to pad their survival meals. They are handpacked right in a family-owned facility in the U.S.A. and you won't believe how inexpensive these kits are.
* There's no fancy packaging, it's sturdy, triple-layer stuff and can stand up to most of the crazy things that happen in a crisis. This food **is designed to last for 25 years** , so you have complete peace of mind for the long term. And they're using compact kits so you can store them anywhere in your house without any extra hassle. They're sturdy, water-resistant and stack easily. And extremely covert too.
* You can make these meals in less than 20 minutes. Just add boiling water, simmer, and serve.
* **Customers rave about the delicious flavor of this food – it doesn't taste like "survival food."** And you get a whole slew of choices for breakfast, lunch and dinner so you don't get stuck eating the same thing day-in and day-out.

# These Survival Food Kits Are
Famous for Selling Out...
Now They're Back in Stock!

body:

---
| | Dear Fellow Conservative, Gas prices have risen dramatically since the Biden administration took office, and I’m sure you’ve felt the pinch at the pump just like the rest of your fellow Americans. There’s no doubt in my mind that our nation’s citizens are in need of relief. But instead, President Biden has throttled U.S. energy production. And he has trapped much of our nation’s vast energy reserves behind a wall of bureaucratic red tape. That’s why I’m sending you this email today. I’d like to hear how the Biden administration’s energy policies have affected you and your family.
---

subject: Where should I send this?
body:
Today, I’m writing to let you know about a special offer for you from Hillsdale College, a private, fiercely independent, cons
[View this message in your browser](http://e.americandefensenews.com/rd/9z2z88u5iv9vrcu59tg5csc55318k1516q95olad8uo_rp2ash2s8gb2sbgebjqf99jkgqracjkmoq37c9l6cqj7djl6eqjetjjkorrbdt469r31d5jekrq9onicbor4opgm5p62opkmaqe6dbgehq78d1kseqb0phmm9gr7dr3chq72or7cjijeclgsv970sj46oob4pjiegonco70mhij1o5l65iecpj666pbbdhnm9o6cdj2mmq6fc5lclp238h1e8he4933cljmcor0c3jm1oh5u)
This sponsored message from American Defense News is on behalf of Hillsdale College [](http://e.americandefensenews.com/rd/9z2zs6v1jfo2t00obcsuofecnnpgvoi153tp07u2bkg_rp22sh2s8i66pb7cphm0o77c3h2bs) Dear Fellow American, I hope you are having a fine day so far. Thanks for opening my email. Today, I’m writing to let you know about a special offer for you from Hillsdale College, a private, fiercely independent, conservative college located in south-central Michigan where I work. You may have heard of us on talk radio, social media, YouTube, podcasts, or even on _Fox News_ because of our educational outreach efforts on behalf of liberty—like our free online courses. __ And though we’re best known for educating Americans about the Constitution and founding principles through these free courses, one of our most popular courses has been about a famous _British_ author—C.S. Lewis. In fact, our free online course, **“[ An Introduction to C.S. Lewis: Writings and Significance,](http://e.americandefensenews.com/rd/9z2zgh0nr4celoqjsd644efplesh59maioh195lliko_rp22sh2s8i66pb7cphm0o77c3h2bs)” **has more than 500,000 people enrolled. C.S. Lewis was a war hero, a bright light during some very dark hours in world history. He is one of my absolute favorite authors because... ...While you may know him for his landmark _Chronicles of Narnia_ series, he was also a brilliant philosopher, writer, and Christian apologist. That’s why we decided to develop this online course. Since launching **“[An Introduction to C.S. Lewis: Writings and Significance](http://e.americandefensenews.com/rd/9z2z6dp31u96u44llbmrlcqeoslth2ivud67r03ta0o_rp22sh2s8i66pb7cphm0o77c3h2bs),” **we’ve received an outpouring of thanks for offering a course that covers the breadth and scope of Lewis’ writings. [](http://e.americandefensenews.com/rd/9z2z098otfrg6sghsmpn937g7foe92qn64n9dno1bqg_rp22sh2s8i66pb7cphm0o77c3h2bs) We’ve also heard from many of our friends that they would love to own a DVD version. So, I’m pleased to announce that the C.S. Lewis course is available on DVD for your home library. You can also give it as a gift or use it in a small group, such as a Bible study or book club. We only made a few of these special sets, so get this premium edition of **“[An Introduction to C.S. Lewis: Writings and Significance](http://e.americandefensenews.com/rd/9z2zdnsciujf0vg8e36jfsn519ch6116ds94pcg6o88_rp22sh2s8i66pb7cphm0o77c3h2bs)”** on DVD today. Given how popular this course is, the DVD box set won’t last long. Reserve your DVD set now using this secure link, while they last: [**https://secure.hillsdale.edu/be-the-first-to-get-hillsdales-new-c-s-lewis-dvd-set/**](http://e.americandefensenews.com/rd/9z2z8dul4n55nluk1hae4n2ke1dg0nlqo0rgrti235g_rp22sh2s8i66pb7cphm0o77c3h2bs) Warm regards, Bill Gray Class of 2001 Associate Vice President of National Donor Outreach Hillsdale College

name: Great America Update
subject: Americans find this “British Bulldog” captivating
body:
You can now own one of Hillsdale College’s most popular online courses, “Winston Churchill and Statesmanship,” on DVD. [](http://click.greatamericaupdate.com/Pages/BC.aspx?ghff34ghj=41%7c612934%7c6124%7c6%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz%7c1&wdmdk=Lk4vrqD8HaEN6YbMbBlBRQ&sysid=1)
This sponsored message from Great America Update is on behalf of Hillsdale College [](http://click.greatamericaupdate.com/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=41%7c63333%7c612934%7c1%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=a7eIH6fTWvRshtmDuLpMTA&sysid=1) Dear Fellow American, You can now own one of Hillsdale College’s most popular online courses, **“Winston Churchill and Statesmanship,”** on DVD. Thousands of people have enjoyed this in-depth course online since its release, _**and now you can add it to your personal library or give it as a gift.**_ In recent years Winston Churchill, who is best known for his leadership during World War II, has been portrayed in numerous films and television shows, proving without a doubt that Americans find the “British Bulldog” captivating. The reasons for this sustained interest in Churchill are profound. No statesman has better diagnosed the threat of modern technology; no statesman has spoken more effectively in opposition to socialism; and, no statesman has displayed more courage in the face of tyranny. It should come as little surprise that so many “academics” and activists are working to smear his legacy, which makes studying a true account of Churchill’s accomplishments more important than ever. **For precisely this reason, I am so excited to offer you this powerful DVD set:** **_[https://secure.hillsdale.edu/winston-churchill-dvd-offer/](http://click.greatamericaupdate.com/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=41%7c63334%7c612934%7c1%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=7AadxhuXwlKb708BTtpeWQ&sysid=1)_** As a student of Dr. Arnn myself, I know that his lectures on Churchill will teach you more about this subject than any of the many books and videos available to you, and they will reveal profound lessons that can be applied to the urgent problems of today. [](http://click.greatamericaupdate.com/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=41%7c63333%7c612934%7c1%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=a7eIH6fTWvRshtmDuLpMTA&sysid=1) Only a limited number of these DVD sets are available, so I hope you’ll take this opportunity to add it to your personal library or give it as a gift--before they’re gone. Here’s a secure link to reserve your copy of **“Winston Churchill and Statesmanship** ” on DVD: _[**https://secure.hillsdale.edu/winston-churchill-dvd-offer/**](http://click.greatamericaupdate.com/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=41%7c63345%7c612934%7c1%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=s7uXvHBH%2fn7puLdBvod63A&sysid=1)_ You’ll undoubtedly enjoy this close study of Churchill’s words and deeds.

Best regards,

Kyle Murnen, ‘09 Director of Online Learning Hillsdale College |
---

body:
|
---
| | | Peter,

We want you to meet **John James, Wesley Hunt, and Tom Barrett.** As former Army helicopter pilots with over 30 years of combined service, they are some of the best our country has to offer.

But they've been called up out of retirement for one final mission. And it's not on a far-away battlefield, but right here at home.

Their mission? Get elected to Congress and help Conservative Republicans save America from the Democrats running it into the ground.

**[Will you help John James, Wesley Hunt, and Tom Barrett save our country? SIGN HERE by 11:59 PM to endorse their campaigns.](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/60760871:6NuLzRoEN:m:1:3253819041:00C65E821C9FACF78FBBF715FA3305BD:r "Will you help John James, Wesley Hunt, and Tom Barrett save our country? Conservative Veterans")
---
The number of Americans who serve our country is at a record low. And even worse, the number of Americans who are proud of our country is at a record low.

Democrats welcome the decline of American patriotism because they want to replace it with support for woke, big government socialism.

But these Conservative vets totally disagree and are getting back into the fight for America's future because they know we must always put our love of country and Conservative values first.

Conservative Veterans")

This mission will not be easy. The Radical Democrats are spending millions of dollars to defeat our America First candidates. They will do anything it takes to stop us.

If the Democrats win and our veterans lose, you will see America decline:
* Your children and grandchildren will have less opportunity than you once did
* The Constitution will become a distant memory
* Our national security will totally fall apart

That's why we're asking you for your endorsement. The future of the country is on the line, but you have a chance to do something about it.
**[CLICK HERE to endorse John James, Wesley Hunt, and Tom Barrett and save our country>>>](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/60760871:6NuLzRoEN:m:1:3253819041:00C65E821C9FACF78FBBF715FA3305BD:r "CLICK HERE to endorse John James, Wesley Hunt, and Tom Barrett and save our country

But they've been called up out of retirement for one final mission. And it's not on a far-away battlefield, but right here at home.

name: AOTL
subject: Pattern Recognition
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE NATIONAL REDISTRICTING ACTION FUND
body:
|
---

Friend --

The radical conservative majority on the Supreme Court has officially stolen away the right to an abortion. The decision -- made possible by Trump’s three appointees -- now leaves this fundamental right up to state legislatures.

More than 60% of Americans believe that abortion should be legal in all or most cases. In 29 of 50 states, a clear majority of people hold this view.

And yet, experts predict that 26 states are likely to criminalize abortion. That shouldn’t happen if state legislatures actually represent the will of the people.

**And now, conservative justices on the U.S. Supreme Court have agreed to hear a North Carolina case that tests a new legal “theory.” The nonsensical “independent state legislature theory,” could give state legislatures complete control of congressional redistricting and federal elections. Under their theory, it is possible that neither governors, nor courts, nor independent commissions get any say in the process.**

**Are you sensing a pattern?**

One can only imagine what our fundamental rights will look like if we lose this fight. Shameless Republican state legislatures will create maps that exclude marginalized communities and pack them into districts to dilute their vote, cementing their ability to create laws that go against the will of the people.

[**We must protect our rights by ensuring that state legislatures accurately reflect the will of the people. Our movement can play a role in protecting our basic human rights by fighting to have state legislators elected in fair districts -- can you make a contribution and help power our campaign for fair maps and elections?**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/47583678/354878442/2050588620?refcode=20220709_AOTL_F_redux_roe_redlite&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjdhYzUzNjRhLTc2ZmYtZWMxMS1iNDdhLTI4MTg3OGI4M2Q4YSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=PB_Ctoc7E8sWsUk9ESVaxsivigdixPU6j3pZ5Lq_mtg=)
